This explains a “curious duality” in these problems for the (self-dual) erasure channel observed by Martinian and Yedidia [Allerton 2003; arXiv:cs/0408008] and partly anticipates recent results on optimal lossy compression by polar and low-density generator matrix codes.

Abstract: In this paper, we present an algorithm to obtain the posterior beliefs of the transmitted bits in an uplink coded massive multiple-input-multiple-output (MIMO) wireless communication system with low resolution analog-to-digital-converters (ADC) at the base station (BS). Further, in coded communications, it is more important to obtain soft symbol estimates for the data bits, rather than hard decisions on the data symbols. We approximate the exact posterior distribution with a fully factorized distribution used in mean field theory in statistical physics, and find its parameters such that the evidence lower bound (ELBO) is maximized. Abstract: Fountain codes are a modern class of error-correcting codes with a reasonably low encoding/decoding complexity and an ability that constitutes their rateless property to generate encoded symbols on demand. Fountain codes have found various applications, most notably in the areas of network communications, data storage systems, delay sensitive transmissions and continuous data delivery. Furthermore, fountain codes form the core part of alternative communications protocols, also known as a digital fountain communication protocol and its modifications, for future networks. Luby Transform (LT) codes are the first practical realization of fountain codes for binary and packet erasure channels. LT codes and the fountain codes that originate from LT codes (LT-based codes), such as Raptor or Zigzag Decodable fountain codes, utilize monomial distributions for degree selection, also known as degree distributions. Degree distributions have a significant impact on the overall efficiency of the fountain codes; therefore, we propose a novel technique that uses deep reinforcement learning to approximate/learn an optimal degree distribution (ODD). We obtain an approximation of an ODD, which we call a learned degree distribution (LDD), and evaluate the fountain codes with the LDD and other previously proposed degree distributions and observe improvements in their performance whenever the codes use the LDD.

The basic idea of the work is to refresh data when the accessed data has a long decoding latency. Abstract: Visible Light Communication is a promising technology for data transmission, which has become more and more relevant in recent years. As outlined in earlier papers, the DaViD (Data Transmission Using Video Displays) system provides a modulation concept allowing for a data overlay onto video content which is nearly invisible to the viewer, i.e. causing hardly any perceptible degradation in video quality. This is achieved by differentially modulating the chrominance information of the underlying video frames with low amplitudes. The resulting color differences within the recorded images can be interpreted by the receiver, allowing for reconstruction of the transmitted data. Abstract: In fifth-generation (5G) wireless networks, the perspective of very large number of simultaneous connections is challenging. Abstract: Replication and redundancy are the two most popular approaches to defend against the worsening reliability problems found in modern storage systems. With the replication approach, one or more copies (“replicas”) of each block are created to guarantee that at least one correct copy is available when failures of single blocks occur. On the other hand, with the redundancy approach, various error correction code (ECC) are employed to compute the redundancy information of data, which can later be used to recover the correct data on potential errors. In practice, on most existing designs of block-based file systems, to alleviate the management burdens of variable-size redundancy information in fixed-size blocks, multiple data blocks are often grouped together for redundancy computation, with their redundancy information stored in the same blocks. While the replication approach incurs higher space wastes, the redundancy approach might degrade the performance of random data accesses, because the associated blocks with redundancy information need to be read along with the blocks with user data. Based on this observation, existing proposals intend to classify files according to whether they are more likely to be sequentially or randomly accessed. However, in certain types of files with hybrid contents, e.g., virtual machine images, it is often difficult to clearly identify whether replication or redundancy approach should be applied to the file. In this work, we propose a hybrid approach between replication and redundancy, where different parts of a file can select different approaches, and even the same parts of a file can use both approaches to reduce management overheads. The proposed approach is then verified through a series of evaluation studies, where the results are quite encouraging.
